review: 미니 게임 천국 같은 느낌으로 이것저것 할 수 있어서 좋아요~

这是一篇长评了，所以我先把结论放前面：
丛林DLC很适合喜欢《潜水员戴夫》本体的玩家入坑。丛林DLC加入后，游戏整体仍然是那个日常休闲放松、节奏缓慢舒适的模拟经营(兼养成)游戏。
入坑后慢慢玩即可，游戏内追赶节奏并不能节约多少现实中的时间，而慢慢玩并不会错过什么收集要素(例外仍然只有本体主线“潜入蓝海团”)。
插播一条提示：丛林DLC必须使用主线通关的存档进入（老存档再过“半天”就能进了）。强烈建议自己体验主线，不推荐在“新游戏”中选择“跳至丛林DLC”（需要跳主线不如导入其他玩家的存档），因为那本质上是读取一个官方特制存档（类似一个慢节奏打通主线的存档，但是【屏蔽了成就获取】）。


【下文轻度剧透，谨慎观看】


丛林DLC的快/慢节奏玩法，体验差异其实仅在很前期，一旦做出电磁催眠枪（丛林枪网枪的下路分支进化），世界线就很快收束了。因为丛林DLC有一个地位独特的料理——鳄鱼塔可，类似本体的“泔水套”（华美特级寿司套餐）。它们的共同点是，一旦到了一定时间节点就能轻易量产，售价还中等偏上，非常护肝，别说用到通关，用到后期图鉴收集阶段都没问题。
（鳄鱼在浅水区固定位置刷新，一旦有了电磁催眠枪（可连射、造价较低），贴近鳄鱼连射4发以上（弹药和氧气非常充裕，打多/打空几发或受伤一两次完全不碍事），稍后鳄鱼就会睡眠并被送上无人机，而烹饪鳄鱼肉/尾巴的其他辅料更容易购买/派遣大量获取。）
然而区别在于，泔水套约第14天才解锁，鳄鱼出场时间却实在太早。再加上“创新”的丛林四合一枪相当于拥有超大弹夹，恰好能在前期清空2-3次全弹夹刮痧刮死鳄鱼，这导致好多玩家思路被带歪了，误以为前期不打鳄鱼开荒肯定很坐牢。


慢节奏真的会坐牢吗？其实不会。我们玩游戏时容易忘记现实中时间的流逝，毕竟我们会代入主角视角，且丛林DLC真有时间流逝机制。潜一次水在时间表上只是一小格，但是占用的现实时间可能很长——尤其是用低配武器清空弹夹刮痧血厚的鳄鱼、大鱼时。（丛林探险也同理）精打细算鏖战三场度过一“天”，现实耗时可能都超过休闲度日三“天”了。
最终反倒是“混日子”享受村庄日常，卖些便宜菜、给村民廉价礼物（浅水区遍地的螺壳、苔藓团子就行），潜水就安全水域避战遛弯捡捡垃圾（危险大鱼的领地过于明显），不仅体验舒适愉悦，实际上也没慢多少。


我们一开始误会丛林DLC的设计了，制作团队并没有故意给我们上强度，虽然水下数值有所膨胀、缝合小游戏存在感有所提高，但是丛林DLC的加入，并没有让游戏本体脱离之前的日常休闲放松、节奏缓慢舒适的模拟经营(兼养成)体验。
但是^2，被误会可不代表这DLC设计失误小。即使只看补丁修复（翻译问题和部分细节问题）后，丛林DLC的评价还是远低于本体。
我感觉设计失误，最主要的点在【引导不足】。单论各种“玩法”的文字说明书（游戏内可以用戴夫的手机重看），似乎没啥问题。但是扛不住丛林DLC的缝合程度比本体高太多了，导致玩家需要频繁地学习新要素新操作，太“忙碌”了就容易忘或忽略信息。

说到“忙碌”，【时间流逝】系统真“功盖天地”了。我敢说丛林DLC超过一半的紧张感、压迫感，都来自右上角那个滴滴转的钟表。我就想知道，这样设计比本体那种半天一回合的“回合制”体验好在哪了？我玩《火山的女儿》一回合内仗着时间不流逝到处找NPC交流，可是玩爽了的。（我理想中丛林DLC的一天，类比本体回合制的话，应该这样安排：早上逛村、上午潜水；中午逛村、下午潜水；傍晚开店、晚上逛村。其中一次潜水可替换为丛林探险、逛村阶段需手动结束。）

其实吧，每天的时间非常充裕，“看开了”的慢节奏玩家知道今天干不完的事明天都会刷新，熟悉流程的快节奏玩家则真能一天干完所有关键工作。那么，初见者呢？入门游戏体验呢？总不能要求所有玩家入坑之前先在网上找攻略学习吧？出必买的玩家，不看攻略直接开玩的玩家，他们是对制作组非常信任，应该是宝贵的资源的。本体【引导】比较好，0基础自己玩体验是不错的；而丛林DLC，【引导不足】太明显了。


【时间流逝】+【引导不足】，组合拳带来误会，导致丛林这个轻松休闲、趣味十足、量大管饱的优质DLC优点有些被埋没了，但只能说制作组有咎由自取的成分。我在很早之前就主张让新人放下强度/进度焦虑，以休闲放松为先，进而充分体验休闲放松的冒险、经营与养成。现在有了新DLC丛林的体验，我更需要把这种主张传递开来了。

不錯啦，但小遊戲太多，煩瑣的事也太多吧。另外，主線的故事不知為什麼後面推進得好快，快到都feel不到原來已經去到結局。去到湖最深處都沒有太大地方可以探索。

話雖如此，都感受製作組對此DLC的誠意，DLC也不貴，整體都是好玩的。 7/10
虽然在大量的梗里找到了少量的游戏，但是依旧很好玩
Buenisimo DLC vale cada centimo

制作组能出DLC我是值得赞扬的，但是制作组在DLC选择了时间系统，让玩家疲于奔命，本作DLC没有养鱼，鱼的素材很少，同时这一作同时有丛林探险和下海捕鱼两个路，但丛林探险的游戏体验挺烂的，要猛猛清怪，战斗系统又体验很一般，把丛林的经历下放到远古水下恐龙世界做文章会更好一点。喜欢玩这游戏的都是喜欢休闲的，但是搞太多东西显得很累，有水果有进丛林探险，有下海抓鱼，目前就下海抓鱼是玩的爽的。斗甲虫是真的赤石体验，初体验之后完全不想抓这个。全村祭礼那一段，可真难为音游苦手了，很多玩的很别扭的小游戏，但总体是好的

Es excelente como cambiaron tanto el juego con solo un dlc! esta increible ,es una nueva experiencia totalmente distinta ,pero que mantiene la escencia! es como que cruzaran stardew valley con dave the diver! impecable,estoy super entretenido!
有做成神作的底子，愣是做成了一个大杂烩，什么元素都沾一点，但个玩法都不愿意（也可能就是没有这个能力）做深入。本体和DLC相当于两个半成品，明明有很棒的游戏底子。却做成了如此平庸的作品。能想到这么做游戏的制作团队也是神人了
review_ko: 명작이 될 잠재력이 있었지만, 억지로 잡동사니로 만들었습니다. 모든 요소를 조금씩 건드리지만, 어떤 게임 플레이도 깊이 있게 만들고 싶어 하지 않습니다(또는 그럴 능력이 없을 수도 있습니다). 본편과 DLC는 두 개의 미완성 작품과 같습니다. 분명 훌륭한 게임 기반이 있었는데도 불구하고, 이렇게 평범한 작품을 만들었습니다. 이런 식으로 게임을 만드는 제작팀도 대단한 사람들입니다.

review:
本体可以给个7~8分，而dlc最多6分。

如果说本体的鲛人村又臭又长，那么相比本体，这个dlc制造了大量冗余的操作，并提升游戏本身难度来消耗玩家时间，从而掩饰dlc本身内容的匮乏。非常的匮乏。
review_ko: 본편은 7~8점을 줄 수 있지만, DLC는 최대 6점입니다. 본편의 인어 마을이 지루하고 길었다면, 이 DLC는 본편에 비해 많은 불필요한 조작을 만들고 게임 자체의 난이도를 높여 플레이어의 시간을 소모하게 함으로써 DLC 자체 콘텐츠의 부족함을 감춥니다. 매우 부족합니다.

review: The English AI translations are extremely noticeable, in both the cold manner of speech and the many grammatical errors. The way characters speak tends to be choppy, lacking in punctuation, will use singular forms instead of plural forms, etc. Please hire **real** translators with your next project! AI cannot adequately replace humans.
review_ko: 영어 AI 번역은 차가운 말투와 많은 문법 오류 모두에서 매우 눈에 뜁니다. 캐릭터들이 말하는 방식은 종종 끊기고, 구두점이 부족하며, 복수형 대신 단수형을 사용하는 경향이 있습니다. 다음 프로젝트에서는 **진짜** 번역가를 고용해 주세요! AI는 인간을 적절하게 대체할 수 없습니다.

review: 我要玩的是森林潜水而不是神庙逃亡解谜、森林和神庙部分玩下来感觉很受罪，篝火点就不能多个直接回村的选项吗？我打到神庙卡怪以后难道是要我一路走回去？还有最后boss必须要前面刷级才能打过，拿徽章部分搞个突然中陷阱了伤害不够小怪一直叫队友你觉得很幽默是吗？还有你那破沼泽区域进去看看就要被怪围殴，都有逃跑系统了以为是给不想战斗的玩家逃课用的结果逃跑还有失败概率，设计的简直一坨屎玩的非常难受，就森林部分制作完成以后内部真的有试玩过吗？最后我不需要缝合游戏，不要被K,M厂商的有些垃圾游戏解谜元素带偏，我需要的独具“潜水员戴夫”的潜水特色和班桥料理经营的特色。

Wirklich gelungene Erweiterung zum Hauptspiel!
Ich hatte mit deutlich weniger umfang gerechnet, aber habe doch gute 20 Stunden gespielt (Primär für die Hauptstory). 
Sehr gut gefallen hat mir das Dorf als neues Hub in welchem man sich frei bewegen kann und in dem es viel zu entdecken und looten gibt.
Der fangen & verarbeiten-Loop ist wie im Vorgänger spaßig und hält auch an.
Auch hier wieder perfekt fürs Steamdeck geeignet.
Wer das Hauptspiel mochte, wird hier ebenfalls auf seine kosten kommen.

Really wanted to love this but don’t, I really wish the awful rhythm game was optional or had a lower score or something, why is a rhythm game with such a high required score necessary in a RPG??? Really wish I could return this since I’ll never be able to finish it. Not to mention the change in how time works sucks so much it now works like a farm sim and it’s annoying as hell.
review_ko: 정말 좋아하고 싶었지만 그렇지 않습니다. 끔찍한 리듬 게임이 선택 사항이거나 점수가 더 낮았으면 좋겠습니다. 왜 RPG에서 그렇게 높은 점수가 필요한 리듬 게임이 필요한가요??? 절대 끝낼 수 없을 것 같아서 환불할 수 있었으면 좋겠습니다. 시간 작동 방식의 변화가 너무 형편없어서 이제 농장 시뮬레이션처럼 작동하고 정말 짜증납니다.

review:
Un capolavoro di DLC pieno di nuove meccaniche e minigiochi stupendi. Ci sono un sacco di collaborazioni e citazioni che rendono l'esperienza super divertente e piacevole. Il finale è puro cinema. Le cinematiche sono capolavori come nel gioco base anche se secondo me si sono nettamente superati in questo DLC.
SUPER CONSIGLIATO
First game that got me to do to a review 10/10

This is a proper DLC like they used to make, and would be considered a sequel if Mint Rocket had lower standards. It features a whole new town with Stardew valley like relationship mechanics, an rpg-like jungle exploration, and expansion upon the the diving and restaurant mechanics. It was a great continuation of the main game and wholeheartedly worth the asking price. 

All that being said, the DLC stretches the experience this game is able to provide, such that it is simply more of Dave the Diver. The new mechanics are enough to keep it fresh and provide a little seasoning, but it is very much the same game. If you loved base game you will certainly enjoy the DLC, but don't expect anything too different from the original.

如同真的吃了一份劣质寿司一样。
用华美的装盘和还算不错的味道骗我吃完，最后让我窜稀。
我不理解为什么要在一个还算不错的dlc中，塞进一个我能想象到的最差的结局，最差。
完全违背你们所谓的海洋保护balabala的大义，只有令人作呕的恶趣味。
玩到中间的时候我给很多朋友都推荐了这个dlc，现在我要去一个一个找他们道歉了，我撤回这份推荐。
review_ko: 정말 형편없는 스시를 먹은 것과 같았습니다. 화려한 장식과 괜찮은 맛으로 저를 속여 다 먹게 한 다음, 결국 설사를 하게 만들었습니다. 왜 괜찮은 DLC에 제가 상상할 수 있는 최악의 엔딩을 넣었는지 이해할 수 없습니다. 최악입니다. 여러분이 말하는 해양 보호라는 대의명분에 완전히 위배되며, 역겨운 악취미만 있습니다. 중간에 이 DLC를 많은 친구들에게 추천했는데, 이제 한 명씩 찾아가 사과해야 할 것 같습니다. 이 추천을 철회합니다.

review:
It's everything a DtD DLC should be.
Might as well have robbed the devs at $10 too, this is practically DtD2.
초반 버그많다고 해도 만원짜리 DLC에 이정도 분량이면 명절날 할머니집 갔을때 할머니가 준비한 밥상급임 ㅇㅇ
7/10，性价比高内容适中，初次游玩体验的确上头好玩，丛林回合制战斗部分好玩；扣分点在于白天晚上自由活动时间短，前期戴夫走得慢真的不太行，应该改掉；另外真的很想要养殖场加入淡水鱼和鳄鱼（
中规中矩 一开图  一地图的任务  有点反感
one of the best DLCs of all time. beats alot of cozy farming games that exist which is crazy?! I wish it had a game of its own cos it was genuinely so enjoyable!

思ったよりボリューム多くて、ほぼデイブ2だった
ゲームの要素若干迷走しすぎ感はあったけど…

I had a very hard time making a review for the base game too. That one turned out as a positive.
Such a nice concept, and so much totally useless side-struggle. Minigames, whether you want them or not...
Some are kinda decent, but this is a diving/fishing game, where the diving content is excelent, which makes the rest of the stuff kinda boring.

As for the DLC. I have to start with this: If you like the base game you'll really like the excellent DLC as well. Fantastic work, especially the "sun bear" animation for the grill place... jsut excellent.

And yet, there is bug-catching, friend-making, turn-based JRPG, whatever. Not the best.

And my biggest issue with the DLC.
Please don't say, I'm to sensitive or something, I can understand very well, that for some to live, other things have to die. We catch fish, we cook them, and even I an not a vegan or anything.

Bit I found the idea of beetle-fighting a bit over the line. That is just animal cruelty. That is sick and pointless.
And even though I had fun with the DLC overall, and I honestly think they did a good work, I sadly am not able to give a positive review alltogether. Especiall because you force animals to fight animals in the game.
It just has a bad taste.
review_ko: 기본 게임에 대한 리뷰를 작성하는 데에도 매우 어려움을 겪었습니다. 그 리뷰는 긍정적으로 나왔습니다. 정말 좋은 컨셉인데, 쓸모없는 부수적인 고생이 너무 많습니다. 미니게임은 원하든 원하지 않든... 어떤 것들은 꽤 괜찮지만, 이것은 다이빙/낚시 게임이며, 다이빙 콘텐츠는 훌륭해서 나머지 것들은 좀 지루하게 느껴집니다. DLC에 대해서는 이렇게 시작해야겠습니다: 기본 게임을 좋아한다면 훌륭한 DLC도 정말 좋아할 것입니다. 특히 그릴 장소의 "태양곰" 애니메이션은 환상적인 작업입니다... 정말 훌륭합니다. 그런데도 벌레 잡기, 친구 만들기, 턴제 JRPG 등등이 있습니다. 최고는 아닙니다. 그리고 DLC에 대한 저의 가장 큰 문제입니다. 제가 너무 예민하다고 말하지 말아주세요. 저는 어떤 것들이 살기 위해 다른 것들이 죽어야 한다는 것을 아주 잘 이해합니다. 우리는 물고기를 잡고 요리하며, 저도 채식주의자나 그런 것이 아닙니다. 하지만 저는 딱정벌레 싸움이라는 아이디어가 좀 지나치다고 생각했습니다. 그것은 단지 동물 학대입니다. 그것은 역겹고 무의미합니다. 그리고 DLC 전체를 즐겼고, 솔직히 그들이 좋은 일을 했다고 생각하지만, 특히 게임에서 동물들이 동물들과 싸우도록 강요하기 때문에 전체적으로 긍정적인 리뷰를 줄 수 없다는 것이 슬픕니다. 그냥 기분이 나쁩니다.

review:
I've been saying for years that Dave the Diver is my favorite indie game of all time. I had over a hundred hours in the base game, and this DLC took me just over 20 hours to complete all story and side content which I think is a fantastic amount of time for a DLC given the price point.
This DLC offered a bunch of new mechanics, recipes, and minigames that were way different from the base game, and most importantly added a ton of fun memorable new characters. It feels like the animators have really outdone themselves here with the most beautiful pixel art I've ever seen. I even laughed out loud a few times at some of the animations, especially the Grill rank up. Muna quickly became my favorite new character with tons of personality and exceptional upgrade animations (the Matrix gun rack one was my favorite)
Once again, Mintrocket blew it out of the water (literally) and I still hold to this being one of my favorite games ever. Bravo

Such a fantastic surprise! I had the best time playing Dave the Diver - and when I completed the main story and all the achievements, I missed this game so much. I bought this DLC hoping for some nice missions and new items, but it is way more than that. I would rather call it Dave the Diver 2 - it could easily be a whole new game, and that is exactly what I needed. I could list all the lovely ideas and fun improvements, but I won't, because I don't want to spoil the surprise for others - and it is 4 in the morning as I write this review because I have just made the adult decision to save the game and go to sleep. What a gem.
私人垃圾dlc 为了圈钱做出这么依托答辩
review_ko: 개인 쓰레기 dlc 돈 벌려고 이런 똥을 만들다니

review: 愚蠢的小游戏
review_ko: 어리석은 미니 게임

review:
DLC可玩性较高，游戏时长在15h+
优点：
1.玩法很丰富，拓展了回合制玩法的森林区域，水下也有新设计
2.置入了很多小游戏，体验感不错
3.许多联动的小巧思，有一种惊喜感
缺点：
1.前期游戏体验不佳，无员工奥姆上菜十分卡手，水下大型鱼需要两轮弹药以上才能带走
2.NPC好感度后置了部分永久性加成
3.大型鱼肉量减少，没有养殖场，下水需求增加，且实时流逝的时间需要认真归化
总的来说，瑕不掩瑜，牺牲部分轻松性换取的体验感，7/10
딴건 다 좋은데 마지막 똥피하기는 진짜아니다..

[h1]가격을 생각하면 칭찬해주고싶지만
기다린 시간을 생각하면 실망만 크다. [/h1]

본편은 강화연출에서 B급감성으로 큰 호평을 얻었지만
이번작은 레시피개방, 강화연출이 너무 실망스럽고
다른게임들 언급만 많고 본연의 맛에는 집중하지 못한느낌

다른 리뷰에서 말하는 뷔페인데 먹을게없다는 리뷰가 딱 정확함
뭔가 컨텐츠를 여러개 만들어보고는 싶었으나 결국 다 지루하고 재미가 없음

나무 세번 흔들고 세번줍고 이걸 정말 의도한거라고? 싶을정도로 
억지 플레이타임 늘리기가 너무 많음
초반에는 귀찮고 힘든 작업들도 후반으로 가서 성장하면 자동화가 이루어지는게 본편이었는데
이번 DLC는 처음부터 끝까지 불편함

스토리가 급하게 끝난 느낌이라는 리뷰에도 100%동의함.
빠르게 나온 DLC도 아니고 수년을 기다린 DLC인데 칭찬할 부분보다 까고싶은 부분이 많다는게 아쉽다.

주변에 이 게임 재밌다고 해보라며 추천을 정말 많이했는데 이제 못할거같음

review:
叢林DLC不太行
劇情本身就沒多長了，還塞一堆莫名其妙的聯動腳色、塞一堆無意義小遊戲、意義不明的RPG橋段、遺跡探險也是虎頭蛇尾，大幅壓縮潛水探索的環節；

review: 内容很多

I would not recommend this DLC since it will boost its stats and the developers might think that this is the direction of which we want the game to progress - hell naw. The DLC stayed true to the games core and nailed the vibes. There are a lot of new minigames and orients the main gameplay to be on land, decorate the shop, you house. It also adds villagers relationship which I personally am not a fan of but im sure someone out there is happy. There are sooo many references to movies/anime and other games like:
[spoiler] Kaiju N8, Two tap museum, Terraria, One Piece, Cut the rope, Guitar Hero [/spoiler] and im sure theres more. BUT it feels out of touch and they are completely unrelated
+ The new weapon - Jungle gun is awesome. Its a fusion between all of the guns and you can swap between them. I hope if they make Dave The Diver 2 they will include this. 
+ The gameplay can be around 15h+, i didnt want to 100% this and im not that type of player.
+ As i said the overall vibe, animations, characters were nailed from the main game. 
- The many crossovers feel like ad reads in the middle of my game and make no sense to be in the game itself. 
- The bug fighting rock/paper/scissors is awful. MOST of the times the opponent bug is WAAAY to powerful for you. There is an option to collect bugs but having such a bad minigame for them is a big minus
-  As i said i dont like the relationship with the villagers as part of the game. Even if you max it out they give you decoration items which im not a fan of. Im not the "i want a cozy, stylish restaurant" i want it to be efficient (employees, cooks etc)
- Most of the enemies in the jungle feel like damage sponges - hit them until they die and there are no new moves or something similar. There are quite a few variants tho
- Upgrading the gun past choosing the variant is pointless to me. You can complete the DLC without them. The only way to stun fishes to get higher grade is through upgrading the weapons - but again pointless if you do not want to 100% it
- The ending mission is disgusting. I know this game is quirky and all but aint to the level of "spread the dinosaur ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ to get out of him"
review_ko:
이 DLC는 게임의 스탯을 높이고 개발자들이 우리가 게임이 발전하기를 원하는 방향이라고 생각할 수 있기 때문에 추천하지 않습니다. 절대 아닙니다. 이 DLC는 게임의 핵심에 충실했고 분위기를 잘 살렸습니다. 많은 새로운 미니게임이 있으며, 주요 게임 플레이를 육상으로, 상점과 집을 꾸미는 것으로 전환합니다. 또한 마을 주민과의 관계를 추가하는데, 저는 개인적으로 좋아하지 않지만 분명 누군가는 좋아할 것입니다. 영화/애니메이션 및 다른 게임에 대한 수많은 언급이 있습니다:
[스포일러] 카이주 N8, 투 탭 뮤지엄, 테라리아, 원피스, 컷 더 로프, 기타 히어로 [/스포일러] 그리고 더 많을 것이라고 확신합니다. 하지만 동떨어진 느낌이고 완전히 관련이 없습니다.
+ 새로운 무기 - 정글 총은 정말 멋집니다. 모든 총의 융합이며 서로 교체할 수 있습니다. 데이브 더 다이버 2를 만든다면 이것을 포함시키기를 바랍니다.
+ 게임 플레이는 15시간 이상이 될 수 있습니다. 저는 100% 완료하고 싶지 않았고 그런 유형의 플레이어도 아닙니다.
+ 제가 말했듯이 전반적인 분위기, 애니메이션, 캐릭터는 본편에서 잘 살려냈습니다.
- 많은 크로스오버는 게임 중간에 광고를 읽는 것 같고 게임 자체에 있을 이유가 없습니다.
- 벌레 싸움 가위바위보는 끔찍합니다. 대부분의 경우 상대 벌레가 너무 강력합니다. 벌레를 수집하는 옵션이 있지만, 그런 나쁜 미니게임이 있다는 것은 큰 단점입니다.
- 제가 말했듯이 게임의 일부로서 마을 주민과의 관계를 좋아하지 않습니다. 심지어 최대치로 올려도 장식 아이템을 줍니다.

review: The best $10 I've spent in a long time.

[h1] The Good, The Mediocre, and the... Eh? [/h1]

The moment the expansion got released, I ensured that I secured the day 1 DLC so that I can enjoy it in it's non-spoilery immersion. Dave the Diver was a surprise breakout indie game of the year for me last year, even though, yes, I know, it got released in 2023. So, the Jungle, pretty cool addition. Let's go over everything.

[h2] The Good [/h2]
This game is basically Dave the Diver 1.5. You're basically starting from "0" but, the way I see it, it's a fresh new experience all over again. The basic Dave the Diver formula is there. You go diving, fish out whatever, night time, go cook food, earn money, get better items, rinse and repeat till forever. The good news? They've tweaked a lot of components, for better, or worse (but I'll get to that component later). The day goes through a "real time" day and night cycle, although, realistically, a full day lasts about... 5 minutes? Maybe more, maybe less. I've never timed it. The jungle weapon. A great addition, and I love it. Multiple useages for the weapon instead of only relying on one weapon. Upgrading as per normal, but at least you can focus on one of them which you really like instead of getting many, many, many components. New fish are always welcomed, and new bosses, as well as a new region and story, although I wonder if the devs are done with the game (fingers crossed for no). There's also a new friend system where you can gift gifts to people, which in turn, earns you customers and gifts. There's also a new tool which Dave can use. I forgot the name but it's basically a hookshot. 

[h2] The Mediocre [/h2]
Fundamentally, some elements of the game for me are put as a question mark for why it exists like so. A main existing thing is the RPG. Admittedly, I am not a fan of turn-based RPGs. But while I didn't find the combat ground-breaking... I didn't hate it. It acts quite similarly to Yakuza: Like a Dragon (sole reason I got Dave the Diver) but it is its own thing. But... I put into question why it exists. Granted, it's a good way to get some materials, but it just feels... pointless? They give a big area to explore, but majority of the time, you'd be in the temple to progress the story. And once you've reached level 8, there's no point to grind for levels as well. The day and night cycle is also a bit weird. Personally, I found vanilla Dave the Diver to be really fun. Adding the day and night cycle makes things more tense since you kind of have to know what you're doing, but either way, it's... not very challenging? It's kind of hard to describe. 

[h2] The Eh? [/h2]
I love a good crossover. It gives communities a way to get into other communities- the exact reason how I got into Dave the Diver, because of Yakuza... this is too much crossovers for no reasons. With Yakuza, there's reason, it's within reasonable bounds. Godzilla is stretching it but at least it still gets explained. Why does Om Nom, WUWA, Two Point Mueseum exist in the same universe? WUWA doesn't even have a proper explanation, it's just that it exists. He came about, the end. Why?

Overall, if you love Dave the Diver, grab this. Actually, don't just grab this, I'd like to give them the whole wallet. Side note however, I hope Cobra gets a skin in Fortnite. Anyways, high hopes for Bancho game.

review:
So, if I could give this a medium, I would.

First of all, the idea of freshwater fish was really exciting to me - and still is. However, there aren't many available variants (multiple different discus… why?). The aggressive enemy fish are really annoying and seem to be bullet sponges.

Still, for the additional content for the price, I would recommend the DLC.

HOWEVER.

Additionally, there are a lot of mini games. None of them are optional. Most of them are clunky as hell. Go figure how enjoyable that experience was.

What really got me was the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ad-reads every 10 min. There are so many 'crossovers' in this game that literally feel like advertisement, I can barely believe I paid 10 bucks for it. It should have financed itself at that point! Ridiculous! I do not want to be advertised to in a game I PAID for!
Was fun.

review:
马马虎虎，不如本体足够有趣，总体上我依然愿意好评。
DLC的甲虫猜拳蠢的一，找个面板稍微高点的无限出锤子包赢，纯粹的浪费时间；村庄内的香蕉树 橘子树包括菜地内的左右都要自己手动去摇树捡东西，时间还是实时流逝的，早上一起床就要注意安排时间，稍不小心就会错过一次下水机会。
至于不少人诟病的陆上回合制战斗倒是没太多说的，我本身是重度jrpg爱好者，对回合制的接受度比较高，但是神庙内的探索和解密做的不够有趣，谜题也设计的一般，不会让人产生二次探索的欲望，后面去单纯是为了挖矿。
本体虽然缝了养殖 种植 开店经营的部分，但是核心还是下海抓鱼 探索深水地形；DLC在这方面继续深入个人认为会更好玩，通过隧道穿越至远古深海居然只是粗粗看了一眼实在太可惜，完全可以继续强化装备（比如和科技公司CEO店员合作），继续做水下生态。
For the cost of this dlc I 100% can say its worth getting. I was missing Dave the Diver and am so glad we got more! We can only hope for Dave the Diver 2 some day or more DLC.

1）丛林融了很多元素，看起来诚意满满，但很多只是出现一次当个小游戏就结束了，真的有这个必要吗？而且加入了非常大量但浅显的联动，除了让我感叹制作组人缘真好之外，只令人感觉增添了无意义的负担。
2）素材的收集非常的繁杂，采集果实甚至有三次毫无必要的前摇，素材散布各处，很给人增负。三个NPC满好感之后每天送一个素材也聊胜于无吧，不如改成农夫可以帮忙种地，两小孩可以帮忙收集村子里的食材。
3）最让我觉得必须要差评的点——结局。[spoiler] 一定要炸死龙王鲸，柯布拉一定要被咬断一条手。 [/spoiler]只能让我感觉是制作组自己的恶趣味，甚至和本体传达的思想都天差地别。“优秀的创作者需要懂得克制”的含金量还在上升。
review_ko:
1) 정글은 많은 요소를 융합하여 성의가 가득해 보이지만, 많은 것들이 한 번만 미니게임으로 등장하고 끝나버립니다. 정말 그럴 필요가 있었을까요? 그리고 매우 많지만 얕은 연동이 추가되었는데, 제작진의 인맥이 정말 좋다는 감탄 외에는 무의미한 부담만 더하는 느낌입니다.
2) 재료 수집이 매우 번거롭습니다. 과일을 채집하는 데 심지어 세 번이나 불필요한 선행 동작이 있고, 재료가 여기저기 흩어져 있어 부담을 줍니다. 세 NPC의 호감도를 최대로 올린 후 매일 재료 하나를 주는 것도 없는 것보다는 낫겠지만, 농부가 밭일을 돕고 두 아이가 마을의 식재료를 수집하는 것을 돕도록 바꾸는 것이 더 나을 것 같습니다.
3) 제가 가장 나쁜 평가를 줄 수밖에 없다고 생각하는 점은 바로 결말입니다. [스포일러] 반드시 용왕고래를 폭파시켜 죽여야 하고, 코브라는 반드시 한쪽 팔이 잘려야 합니다. [/스포일러] 이것은 제작진의 악취미로밖에 느껴지지 않으며, 심지어 본편이 전달하는 사상과도 천지차이입니다. “뛰어난 창작자는 절제할 줄 알아야 한다”는 말의 가치는 계속해서 상승하고 있습니다.

tl;dr It's Dave the Diver, DLC feels like standalone content, tons of cameos, with about as much content as base game up to the Sea Person Village. Worth 10$ easily, I want Dave the Diver 2.


This DLC is perfect if you enjoy Dave the Diver's base game, it's more of the goofy shenanigans, fun minigames and diving that made the game so addicting back when it originally released. It also features a ton of Cameos as you'd expect, which not everyone will love, although it is fun playing Who's who with your selection of weird crossover staff members.

I'd personally treat this DLC as a standalone as it has very little interaction from the base game. Beyond it carrying over whatever Diver Charms you've hoarded and your iDiver upgrades, nothing else you've built up from the Blue Hole will be used for this DLC.

Along with keeping this DLC relatively standalone, it seems the team at Mintrocket are using this DLC as a chance to try and refine some of the mechanics from the base game, which allows for a more positive experience, as far as I've seen. The biggest changes come from the introduction of the Swiss-Army Jungle Gun, which gives you more options with diving, the addition of Unique Staff skills that diversifies your staff further than those of the base game, and the addition of the village hub/jungle expedition, where you can collect more ingredients and do tasks for your neighbors for some unique rewards.

My only real complaint from the DLC is that I wish there was more to it, it's definitely worth the 10$ spent, but after replaying through the entirety of the base game to re-familiarize myself before I hopped into the DLC naturally, I can only say that I hope to see a true sequel in the works at some point, or for more meaty content to be added in, preferably with it being original, or if it's another crossover, give it some heft, like the Dredge DLC has.

review: 好評，但對於最終主線沒有任何提示，有些魚還沒收集到，結果主線過完了，導致古生物都沒了無法收集，明明整個都玩得挺開心，結果最後被弄得有點不爽。

Overall, I like the DLC and would recommend it to others, but I think the devs went a bit overboard in places they shouldn't have, and didn't concentrate on parts of the game they should have. I would have preferred a more paced story in place of the crazy amount of minigames they had in this DLC. While they were all fun, I played most of them only once since there's no benefit to playing them when it comes to progression or upgrades, and it felt like more of a distraction to what was otherwise a good story. They throw so much at you in this DLC that it does seem to overshadow the main story, and then the main story ends and you're kind of like... hmmm, okay that's it? I think if they wouldn't have put so much time into all the minigames, they could have put more effort into the main story.

The dining part of the DLC seems kind of meaningless and overly easy compared to the base game. I was able to get thru the entire DLC without a single negative customer. I felt no real need to ever train my employees past 3 or 4. There was also no need to use special recipes (which there were tons), since highly upgraded base fish meat from the bigger fish would carry you thru the game. Again, I blame all of this on the pacing of the game. So while the dining portion of the base game was very important, it feels like an afterthought in the DLC, something the devs felt needed to be in the game, but something they didn't give much attention. There was only one special customer quest I got during the game, I get that it is a DLC, but just one?! Things like that were missing from the DLC, and seemed to be replaced with minigames filler. 

I did love the RPG turn based fighting they added, but again with the pacing, it feels like it's over before it really starts. They added all these things to the DLC, but then they don't really use any of them. They're all fun individually, but it doesn't seem there was much thought given to how they should work together. It feels like the game should have been longer, and tons of parts were cut to meet a deadline. Feels like a lot of these things were supposed to work together, but they needed another year to do it. The huge boar is a good example. Where did that come from? It felt so forced and out of nowhere. Was this supposed to be a boss originally? I got that feeling a lot in this game, that I'm missing something, or something was cut out of the story. And it would make sense with the pacing complaint I have. The game didn't have to be this rushed, and it doesn't feel like it was designed to be rushed, but it was. There was no real point to gifting anything to the villagers, or increasing how much they like you. The only time I think their like of me came into the game was when we needed to move the stone, but, when that came time, all I had to do was walk around the village and find people. Everyone already liked me enough to get the job done, so again, pacing, I feel like there was no real effort required from me there, and it's like this throughout the entire game. I get that some people hate grinding, but there is literally zero grind in this game at all. Zilch, but it also seems like it was designed around a bit of a grind. 

Again, after all the complaints above, I would still recommend it to others, as it was fun and well worth the money Mintrocket is asking for it, but the pacing is definitely off.

I don't understand the complaints about the new real-time mechanics. I didn't find this to be a detriment to the game. It's just different. Overall DLC was better than I expected and estimate I got almost 15 hours of content. Look forward to this studio's next project. If this is what they can pull off with DLC, I'm especially excited for what a true sequel will look like!

PROS:
- 12-15 hours of quality creative new gameplay (also not a completionist). YMMV.
- Surprisingly fresh game. I was worried it would just be a reskin of the OG but they put in so much work this felt like it could have been an entire sequel to the original.
- Pretty much more of everything you loved from the OG, without too much repetition. They streamlined more of the diving part, which I think was a wise design decision. Jungle Gun very convenient. No more tedious navigation underwater in the new map.
- Inventory markers for appropriate gifting.
- Many other creative and cool surprises.

CONS:
- Unplayable on the Steam Deck due to poor framerates. The 3D village is surprisingly punishing and hope they figure out how to optimize it. For the graphics, 1080p/60 should be sustainable.
- Beetle battles are pure RNG which is little annoying, but skipping it entirely didn't detract from the game.
A lot of good content

It's when you try to do too much and end up with an inferior product to what the original Dave the diver is. I wanted to like it, and in some regards I do, but I just feel the main story was way too short, (I couldn't actually believe how short), way too many crossovers that just end feeling like you stray way too far from the original concept. Way too many boring, useless mini games. Lots of tasks are tedious and slow, the progression system and upgrades don't make sense, like for instance the filter that helps you breathe. You upgrade it only to use it for a very limited time as you eventually don't need it anymore. A lot of surface activities that really don't belong in a game about diving that we all came to love. Exploring forests is not what I would say qualifies for a Dave the Diver game. Rebrand it then to Indiana Dave, or Dave the Jungle Explorer.  All in all its just missing that charm and fun the original has, and that made me pour a substantial number of hours into it. I just feel it's Dave the Diver buried under a mountain of experiments and ideas that don't have anything with the original game, and it just doesn't work. So, Mint Rocket, please come back to the roots of the game and don't stray too far, otherwise we might have another Plant and Zombies scenario, where it all just goes down the drain with every iteration. Cheers

review:
If you loved **Dave the Diver**, this DLC is an easy recommendation.

Rather than feeling like a small content drop, *In the Jungle* plays more like a compact expansion. The new rainforest setting has fantastic music and atmosphere, the villagers are a charming new cast of characters, and I quickly found myself invested in helping them and upgrading the jungle grill. The social system with the villagers ended up being my favorite addition, and the new trun based RPG-inspired sections were a fun surprise that mixed up the usual gameplay loop.

The new freshwater creatures are great too. Familiar enough to fit Dave the Diver, but different enough to keep every dive exciting (the first time I ran into an Electric Eel did **not** end well). The game also keeps its trademark humor, quirky animations, and constant stream of minigames that make you say, "Just one more mission."

It's not perfect. The bosses are a little too easy, some mechanics (like the beetle battles) feel underdeveloped, and the ending wraps up a bit abruptly.

Still, none of those issues took away from my enjoyment. This isn't trying to replace the base game—it's **more Dave the Diver**, just in a tighter, bite-sized package with enough fresh ideas to keep everything feeling new.

**10/10** — not because it's flawless, but because it's exactly what I wanted from a Dave the Diver expansion.

곤충채집도 미니 게임을 넣어서 하는건 좋은데 너무 운빨임. 내가 찾은 방법은 그냥 상대할 곤충보다 더 능력 좋은 곤충 넣고 그냥 방어 제외하면 둘 중에 하나 맞아 떨어지게 끔 딜찍하는게 정답이더라. 운빨요소가 너무 심해서 솔직히 처음은 오오오!!! 하지만 중간가면 아 귀찮아가 됨. 어차피 미니 게임인데 운빨 가위바위보가  아니라 다른 요소를 만들어도 되지 않았을까? 전작의 갸오의 참참참도 운빨이어서 한번 실패하면 두세번을 또 해야하는 귀찮은 요소가 되는데 여기에도 그걸 넣을 필요는 없었다고 봄.

Took all the right steps to move the game forward. Fantastic expansion.

More fish, more bosses, and tweaks on the serving minigame were all expected, but I'm happy to see them still. The jungle gun was a great way to avoid having to design around old weapons/open up new weapon types. A maxed-out hush dart and sniper rifle from the base game would've trivialized everything from the start, and I appreciated seeing the new progression. Swapping weapon types on the fly was also much appreciated. I see lots of reviews complaining about the jungle gun, but overall keeping the expansion content independent from the base game (outside of iDiver levels) was the right call to keep this engaging.

However people who finished the base game (as is required to start the dlc) won't really be shocked or surprised by the combat in the expansion. The caiman, sturgeon, etc aren't any more scary than the base game's white tip, and the new bosses are trivial damage sponges compared to the mechanically demanding bosses of the base game (not that the original bosses were particularly difficult, but they at least made you think). This extends to the RPG jungle combat, where you'll be overleveled for the endgame bosses by about the halfway point of your first visit. The jungle stuff felt like an overstretched minigame rather than a game mode on par with diving (as the framing of it clearly intends). Anyone who's made it past Mt. Moon won't find anything involved or challenging about the encounters.

But this kinda follows along with the excess of minigames that the base game had. There are lots of points in the DLC where you're suddenly asked to play a completely different game, with the same kind of mixed results as in the base game. Bug catching was a standout (beetle battles less so) and relationship management was fun, but things like the [spoiler]Miside[/spoiler] quest felt like total stretches. The collab stuff overall was way more hit-or-miss than the crossovers in the original. The [spoiler]Two-Point Museum[/spoiler] stuff was fine enough as it passively progressed as you played the main game, but who's even thought about [spoiler]Cut the Rope[/spoiler] since 2011? There were a couple other things that I think *might* have been crossovers ([spoiler]the guy from the stuff in the merch store?[/spoiler]), but I'm honestly not sure. Obviously not everything is gonna be a slam dunk like Dredge or Godzilla, but the crossovers in the dlc were definitely getting into headscratcher territory. But overall it's innocuous enough and completely skippable so it isn't a huge ding against the expansion really.

Lots of reviews are complaining about the time management, to which I say: git gud lol, the time limit is more than lenient enough to complete two dives and whatever menu grinding you need to take care of.

Just beat it. Improves upon the original in some ways. Would recommend if you enjoyed the base game. Short, sweet and only $10

Dave the Diver 2. It’s impressive that they just released a DLC that could easily be a standalone game.

This DLC is f*cking awesome and I completely agree with the people who say it feels more like another standalone Dave the Diver game than just a DLC.
The new world is exciting and challenging, the characters are fun, and the quests are engaging without ever feeling frustrating. It also introduces new mechanics that go beyond what the base game offers, making the experience even more enjoyable. Progression feels smooth and straightforward.
The only aspect that might take some getting used to is the new restaurant mechanic. Instead of simply hiring staff every night, you have to manage serving, grilling, and dealing with "special guests" yourself. It can feel a bit overwhelming at first, but it quickly becomes easy and simple.
Please don't listen to the people complaining about the time management. The first day can feel a little restrictive if you are used to the base game's more relaxed pacing but each day gives you more than enough time to gather resources and dive twice. Once you are familiar with the village and know where to find everything, it is completely manageable.

Overall, this DLC captures the same feel-good vibes as the base game while adding plenty of fun twists. Worths every penny.

10/10
It could be a full game Dave the Diver 2 instead of a DLC, but it's still 10/10. Best game of 2026 so far.

kino
Great DLC.

건포도를 듬뿍 담아서 구운 파운드 케이크 같은 느낌

기본적으로 맛있음. 쇼트 케이크가 채워주지 못하는 파운드 케이크 특유의 감성. 여전히 일품임.

다만 맛있는 케이크를 마냥 즐기기에는 건포도를 걸러내야 하는 수고로움을 감수해야 함. 편의성이 그것임.

이동하는 동안 시간이 고정되지 않음에도 굳이 뺑 돌아가야 하는 마을 동선.

쫓기는 시간 속, 기믹을 풀기 위해 상호작용 키를 눌렀지만 반응이 없어 뚱땅거리는 걸음으로 멀리 돌아 다시 상호작용이 되도록 해야 하는 답답함과 짜증.

느리고 답답하고 수고로운 채집 과정. 굳이 채울 필요가 있나 싶은 주민들 호감도 노가다. 빠따마려운 원숭이와 함께 하는 식당 서빙 등.

그 모든 과정이 마냥 괴롭지는 않았지만, 그렇다고 마냥 유쾌하지만도 않았던 것이 곱씹어도 꼭 건포도 같았네요.

그럼에도 재밌게 했습니다.

탐험의 대상이 바다에서 정글의 호수로 좁아진 만큼, 숲을 탐험하고 주민들 한 사람 한 사람과 친해지는 과정은 충분히 만족스럽진 못해도 여러 NPC를 공략해야 한다는 점에서 양적으로는 꽤 풍요로웠네요.

DLC 가격이 12000원이지만 이런 결과물일 줄 알았다면 20000원이 조금 넘었어도 저는 짜증을 내면서도 지갑을 열었을 거 같네요.

며칠 간 잘 즐기다 갑니다.

I love this game dearly and have been enjoying the DLC but I cannot give the game a thumbs up until they bother to do something about the screen shake. It's a basic accessibility feature and it's ridiculous that there isn't an option to turn it off. I could to an extent deal with it in the base game but the DLC has added so much screen shake effects it's become unplayable. If I could refund I would but I've unfortunately put too much time into it I can't. The screen shake effects get more intense when you explore the forest and it takes a little bit to get there. 

On top of that it has been complete radio silence from the devs for adding a screen shake toggle since the original game has come out. No responses on the forums and none in the discord even when seeing the devs and even the director responding to messages. They're going completely ignored. I will update my review if they ever add the feature but I'm not getting my hopes up.

DO NOT GET THIS GAME IF YOU'RE SENSITIVE TO SCREEN SHAKE EFFECTS.
review_ko:
저는 이 게임을 정말 좋아하고 DLC도 즐기고 있지만, 화면 흔들림에 대해 뭔가 조치를 취하기 전까지는 엄지손가락을 치켜세울 수 없습니다. 이것은 기본적인 접근성 기능인데, 끌 수 있는 옵션이 없다는 것은 터무니없습니다. 기본 게임에서는 어느 정도 참을 수 있었지만, DLC는 너무 많은 화면 흔들림 효과를 추가하여 플레이할 수 없게 되었습니다. 환불할 수 있다면 좋겠지만, 불행히도 너무 많은 시간을 투자해서 할 수 없습니다. 숲을 탐험할 때 화면 흔들림 효과가 더 강해지며, 그곳에 도달하는 데 시간이 좀 걸립니다.

게다가, 원래 게임이 출시된 이후로 개발자들은 화면 흔들림 토글 추가에 대해 완전히 침묵하고 있습니다. 포럼에서도, 디스코드에서도 아무런 응답이 없으며, 개발자들과 심지어 디렉터가 메시지에 응답하는 것을 보았을 때도 마찬가지입니다. 그들은 완전히 무시당하고 있습니다. 이 기능을 추가하면 리뷰를 업데이트하겠지만, 큰 기대는 하지 않습니다.

화면 흔들림 효과에 민감하다면 이 게임을 구매하지 마세요.

Ok, let me preference this by saying that I enjoyed the DLC but I think we need to address the elephant in the room. 

The number of crossovers, references, and nonsensical minigames. 

The sheer amount of them in this game really took me out of the story, especially seeing things that drastically stand out in the universe among characters like Mita or Om-Nom. If I wanted to see these characters or play mini-games with them or the game they came from, then I would play THOSE GAMES and not Dave the Diver. 

It really sucks because I love Dave the Diver and enjoyed the DLC, but I'm really sick of the crossovers or unskippable mini-games they keep pushing. Yes, the fans liked the weird and silly stuff in the original game, but now it feels like you found that we liked those things, so now youre forcing it in everything, and it feels bad. It almost feels like you sold out, and whoever pays you to be in the game, you'll have them and add them in. It sucks because if there's more DLC or a 2nd game, I really would like it to not continue going down this path. 

The DLC is fun, but it will wear you out by the end with the amount of references, crossovers, and mini-games.

Overall, I really enjoyed a big portion of this DLC. New area, building friendships with the villagers, collecting bugs and amazing graphics & animations. A nice story with an epic ending with Dave and the Gang. The devs clearly spent a lot of time crafting a DLC that feels like a full story of the base game itself.

But there are a few things that's a bit... odd, shall we say.

It was also offputting to see blatant collabs with multiple games crammed into this DLC. And the myriad of mini games, while at times fun, at times it overstayed their welcome especially when I expected more depths in the questline than "oh it's... another collab with a different game..." it absoutely took me out of immersion from the world of Dave the Diver. Some mini games are bugged as well? I had to redo the Guitar Hero Mini Game 4x just to get enough score to clear without any way to skip, it was very annoying to redo 4 full songs just to get enough score....

All and all, while I did enjoy myself playing this DLC, it feels quite off due to the aforementioned points. I also agree with the other reviewers' criticism. I'm glad Dave the Diver is a very successful game, but the direction of the game's series is....odd. You probably would never see another game's DLC with this many blatant shilling of OTHER games.
